summary: The FK8 Type R benefits from Honda's engineering reputation. The K20C1 turbo engine is robust, but early cars had some build quality concerns and the dual-axis strut design is unique.

issues.name: 1st/2nd Gear Grind
issues.description: Some FK8s develop grinding when shifting into 1st or 2nd gear, particularly when cold. Often related to synchronizer wear or fluid degradation.

issues.name: Turbo Bearing Noise
issues.description: A small number of FK8s develop turbo bearing whine at higher mileage. Honda extended warranty coverage on early production units.

issues.name: Infotainment System Glitches
issues.description: The touchscreen infotainment system can be laggy, freeze, or develop Bluetooth connectivity issues. Software updates help but don't fully resolve.

issues.name: Clutch Damper Spring Noise
issues.description: The clutch pedal can develop a spring noise or rattling sound from the dual-mass flywheel damper. Annoying but not harmful.

issues.name: Paint Quality
issues.description: The FK8's paint — particularly Championship White — is thin and chips easily. Rock chips on the front bumper and hood are extremely common.

buyingTips: The K20C1 engine is very strong — regular oil changes with 0W-20 synthetic every 5,000 miles is key
Test shifting thoroughly — pay attention to 1st and 2nd gear engagement when cold and hot
Check if the car has been modified and returned to stock — the FK8 tuning community is active and detuned cars are common
Look for paint protection film (PPF) on the front end — the thin paint chips very easily
Verify no track abuse — look for excessive brake dust buildup, cracked rotors, and uneven tire wear patterns
